name: SCHATZKI RING
definition: LOWER ESOPHAGEAL MUCOSAL RING constant lower esophageal ring (mucosal thickening) presumed to result from reflux

demographics.Frequency: 6-14% of population; old age > young age; M > F
demographics.Histo: usually squamous epithelium on upper surface + columnar epithelium on undersurface;

discriminating_features: nondistensible concentric transverse ring of shelflike projection with smooth symmetric
visible only with adequate distension of esophagogastric region and when located above the
best demonstrated in prone position during arrested deep inspiration with Valsalva maneuver
short esophagus + intrahiatal / intrathoracic gastric segment = sliding hiatal hernia if Schatzki

general: nondistensible concentric transverse ring of shelflike projection with smooth symmetric
visible only with adequate distension of esophagogastric region and when located above the
best demonstrated in prone position during arrested deep inspiration with Valsalva maneuver
short esophagus + intrahiatal / intrathoracic gastric segment = sliding hiatal hernia if Schatzki

clinical.Cx: impaction of food bolus (associated with severe chest pain)
